title: Whither repeat players? Litigation experience and success in court: Evidence from Russian commercial courts
abstract: This paper tests the party capability theory of Galanter (1974) using the universe of legal entities and their 9.8 million commercial disputes in Russia in 2012-2019. We find that repetitiveness is negatively associated with litigation success in general. We explain this by selection into litiga-tion. Entities engaged in unfair business practices are repeatedly selected into disputes as re-spondents becoming repeat players in spite of themselves. We argue that repetitiveness should be decoupled from party capability and urge for conceptual revision of the notion of a repeat player in court.
authors_text: Volkov, Vadim; Skougarevskiy, Dmitriy; Kuchakov, Ruslan
